english: Dark Tyrant Appearance Package now available!
Darkness closes in. Seek light if the darkness envelops you.

Darkness pays Orr a visit.With billowing robes of blackened silk,She beckons us, arms outstretched.I see my brothers walk forward, greet her as a friend.So many fold themselves into her embrace.And even over their cries, and the roars of the beasts,I hear Darkness call to me with a promise.But I close myself. I will not join her yet.Another call is more beautiful,And I will chase it back to you.

Day 1: New experiment, new journal! Ceara is ready—excited, even! My previous attempts have all ended in failure, but Ceara...if anyone can pierce the veil, it's her. I've made her aware of the possible risks just like the others, but she's still keen to go through with it.    I'm torn. Ceara is the most brilliant student I've had the pleasure to mentor. Her propensity for learning is beyond anything I've seen in the sylvari, but this breakthrough could change everything about how we view the very magical fabric of Tyria!    Day 2: Ceara is in the isolation unit. Her vitals are stable and there's minimal movement. Already a vast improvement over previous volunteers, but I'm still anxious. If I keep squeezing my left ear, it'll bruise for certain. Is this wrong? Should I cut my losses now while she's still whole?     Day 3: Vitals are still stable. She's been writhing for a few hours now, shifting from screams to laughter from moment to moment.    Every genius has their doubts. Moments where they question the worth of their pursuits and their methods. Those who say otherwise are either unhinged or terrible liars. This isn't like Vorpp's work; I didn't kidnap my subjects and torture them. Am I too attached? Too close to this? Maybe. Would Ceara be furious with me if I brought her out too early? Alchemy, I need sleep.    Day 4: Vitals are erratic at times, but nothing I can't correct. My left ear is bruised. I've slept all of three hours. ~~The subject~~ Ceara has exhibited bouts of thrashing and continued emotional instability. There are moments when she'll speak or shout in response to something. None of the previous participants made it this far.     We're on the cusp of a breakthrough, I know it!

DAY 1Dear Blish,I'm recording this for posterity. If I die in here, at least my thoughts will live on, free from the chains of a corrupt system! But I'm getting ahead of myself...Kidnapped by someone I considered a friend. Someone I.. no, it's too much to think about. And now I'm the one behind bars. If there's justice in this world, it's sorely lacking in...wherever I am...It's lonely here. The commander's out cold. And the guards seem irritated by my questions. So I decided to write to the one person I could always talk to. Just wish you were really here. You always knew what to do.—GorrikDAY 3Dear Blish,The commander's still holding on. Today the guards brought in some bizarre device. It seems to be aiding the recovery process. I think you'd find it as fascinating as I do. It emits a green light. Strange, but oddly captivating. I'm writing this entry under its eerie glow. I asked the guards about it, but they weren't too keen on chatting. I'll try to get more out of them tomorrow.—GorrikDAY 7Dear Blish,I'm starting to lose it! I swear the walls are closing in! Don't know how much more I can take of this isolation. Treated worse than an ant searching for a spare crumb or scrap of—Ooooh, lunch is here! Smells delicious. Oh, and they gave me extra fruit on my dessert today! I always like the little flower they put on the serving tray. It's a sweet personal touch.Will write again soon, if I'm not too weak and malnourished to hold my pen.—GorrikDAY 9Dear Blish,You don't think Taimi's mad at me, do you? At least, not that mad. Not if she's too busy being worried about me...But that means I'm making her worry. Somehow that feels worse than making her mad.—GorrikDAY ???Dear Blish,The guards said the commander's condition is improving. Still unconscious, though. This will sound silly, but sometimes, when no one else is here, I chat with the commander. I know it's one-sided, but in a way that makes saying what I really want to easier.I think having the commander as a friend is good for me. Since we started working together, I've felt a little braver. More confident & comfortable. I haven't felt like that since...well, since you were around. I said all this last night in one of our "little chats." I'm glad the commander won't ever know how sappy I sounded. I'd be so embarrassed!Missing everyone extra hard tonight. Especially you.—Gorrik

Day 1It's scary to be down here—everything smells of dust and death and mildew. Even the Durheims seem a little bothered, but they said that we can't cause too much of a fuss, or we'll be found out.Day 5A bunch of dredge arrived today. I've never seen one up close before. They're disgusting, with huge teeth and little squinted eyes. They went around introducing themselves, but I pretended to be asleep.Day 13One of the dredge found a way to open a series of vents to the surface to bring in some fresh air. Nothing's ever smelled so sweet! They also showed us how to clean and bury refuse, and it's already so much cleaner in here. I never thought that creatures who looked like they do, that live in the dirt, could be so clean!Day 24Atrus was killed today. He went out with a group to find food and a patrol got them. He said he'd lead the Ministry brutes off while the rest of the group went another way. They saw him climb a tree and then fell out a few minutes later... Shot with an arrow.One of the dredge, Pavel, brought me some tea and sat with me while I cried. He said his brother had been killed, too. We promised each other we'd make the Ministry pay.Day 31They said the Ministry is closing in on us. The last group left with all the children, taking them to Kaineng, but I argued I was old enough to stay, and Pavel told them to let me. He knew I needed revenge. We're a team now.Day 33Scouts say the Ministry is close. Pavel and I have been training day and night. I was always good with my family's weapon, but with Pavel to hand me arrows, I can hit a target across the room even in near darkness. He says I have the senses of a dredge now.Day 35 They're at our door. Pavel is readying the arrows. He said for every arrow of mine that hits, he'll think of Atrus, and I said I'd think of Czibor. I hope they'll be proud of us when we see them.

Day 1    Aye, maybe we're as doomed as they all say, but we'll not just accept our fates here. Thing is, cobbling together something that'll float ain't an issue—entire shore's littered with shipwrecks, after all. Got an infinite supply of parts! Just don't have an infinite supply of lives...    Day 23    Fair winds today, finally. Rocc managed to get the ship—though might be optimistic calling it a ship, more a raft with a sail if you ask me—out into the bay farther than I could spot him without a spyglass. Thought maybe he was gone forever 'til he showed up at the pub, clothes soaked and in tatters. Said he hit a patch of foul weather just beyond sight of shore, then he had to outrace a shark back to the beach.    Day 51    Few more test runs up and down the coast, all successful. It's when we head east a certain ways, something always happens—swarming crabs, foul weather, thick fog, you name it. But this ship is solid. It's now or never. Rocc and I are going for it. Our lives are back on the mainland, not in this tiny patch of sand. We're going home to our families, or we'll die trying.    Day 52    Rocc here. Don't know what to write. Paldius was the writing type, not me. He didn't make it. Fog was thick as stew. Crabs swarmed us. Couldn't see my own hands in front of my face. When we came back out of the fog—facing west, somehow—Pal was gone. I limped what was left of the ship home.    I'm done. Can't do this anymore. Writing or sailing. Time to admit we're stuck. I'm stuck.    Good-bye, friend.

Day 32—We made camp in Arborstone while we conduct our surveys in Echovald. The Kestrels were hospitable, but distant. I suppose I would be too, with the things they see every day. The gangs grow bolder, the atmosphere more tense. While we are inside the walls of Arborstone, we are looked after by a guardian, Ishan. His presence is a comfort.Day 37—We heard rumors of outsiders arriving in Cantha! I am excited, but Suni is skeptical. She doesn't believe this will be good for our nation. "Just more squabbling for us to deal with," she says. I'm trying to get more details.Day 40—Suni was right about the squabbling! The outsiders have been here less than a week and are already at odds with every faction in Cantha! One group flew over in their crude airship, and we heard rumbles like cannon fire from off to the southeast.Day 41—We just got word about a tragedy in Kaineng! Power outages, strange malfunctions across the board. There are even people claiming to have seen the deep-sea dragon, Soo-Won, with their own eyes—along with another shimmering being in pursuit. Some claim it was a second dragon. Some think it might have been one of the gods, Dwayna, returned from the Mists. Ishan has been acting strangely—his mood has darkened considerably. I can't say he is making me comfortable anymore...
Day 5: I traveled farther inland. The architecture here is far more grandiose. It's truly a sight to behold. I'm amazed that the rise of Orr didn't completely reduce these monuments to rubble.

Day 5    Marnie made breakfast for me again this morning. She says I'm never going to solve my problems on an empty stomach. I don't know if she's right, but her delicious crab cakes certainly aren't hurting anything. If the locals are right about no one ever being rescued off this place—Castora, the locals call it—I have a lot of crab cakes in my future.    I do have a lot of problems in need of solving—I'm still not entirely sure how I landed on this island. Every time I reach for those memories, they dissolve in my mind's eye, leaving voids behind. I used to be a drakologist, studying the Branded as part of the Astral Ward, when—    There they go again. Dissolving.    No one from the ward has tried to contact me, at least not to my knowledge. I haven't been able to do much but eat and sleep. And sometimes even those elude me. I'm sure I look a sight—I haven't showered in days, and my ward uniform is all I've got to wear—but Marnie hasn't mentioned any of that. She's been unfailingly kind and patient, her and that Cassi girl both. I'm not used to being doted on like this. I never was by Isgarren's people...not that I can remember, anyway.    That seer... When I was recruited by the ward, I made it clear that I joined to further my own goals, not just theirs—I made a promise to my people. And it seemed they agreed. But when I questioned my orders, I was informed that Isgarren said my goals would have to "wait indefinitely in the face of more critical matters." I understand his urgency in dealing with the Kryptis, but the abrupt dismissal of my goals felt like a slap in the face.    Marnie and Cassi are calling. Maybe they caught another fish. I wish they weren't the only ones looking—    (The ink runs off the page, as though the author left quickly.)
Daylight is waning. Brace for Mordremoth's nighttime assault.

Dear %str1%,

With you occupying the attention of Scotta's bandits, we've been able to get supplies across the line—enough to offer you a small boon for your efforts. As you continue into the wilderness, I ask only that you consider helping not only the Seraph, but any Wyld Hunt valiants or Peacemakers you encounter. I believe that by working together will we prevail over our common enemies.

—Seraph Watchman Morina

Dear %str1%, It was a sad day today in Winslow Manor. We had to bid farewell to our dear, devoted Mr. Nibbles. When he left us, he took a piece of me with him—specifically, one of my toes. But the domain soldiers on nonetheless. I'm even thinking of planting a garden!All my best to you and yours,Lord Blaine, Archduke of the Well of Winslow

Dear %str1%, We've returned to our camp at Thunder Ridge after unveiling the towering monstrosity at the heart of Viathan Lake. This malignant and unapproachable tower is polluting the region with hallucinatory poisons, and Marjory is hard at work to find a counteragent. If you are able to collect any samples of the toxic spores, please bring them to Marjory.I suspect you're already a part of this, as you aren't the type to stand idly by while others suffer. Provided you aren't too busy foiling enemy raids on resources or pruning the offshoots of their tower that are sprouting up across Tyria, Priory Arcanist Dolja here in our camp could use your help investigating a possible krait motive for allying with the Nightmare Court.Thank you for your continued assistance, my friend.—Lady Kasmeer Meade

Dear %str1%,2020 and 2021 brought extraordinary changes to everyone's lives. We've forged this accessory in honor of the Guild Wars 2 community and the support you've given us through these challenging times. Thank you for playing The Icebrood Saga with us.—The Guild Wars 2 Team

Dear %str1%,

Please join us at the Dead End bar in Divinity's Reach for a celebration of our victory over Scarlet. We'd really love it if you could attend. Rox and Braham have already sent an RSVP in the form of a bottle of charr whiskey and a keg of norn beer. You don't have to bring anything but your smile. Though you'll probably want to wear a bit more than that.

—Kasmeer

Dear %str1%,

The krait in Slaver's Deeps have their tails between their legs. They won't be hassling us with as much vigor again any time soon. I just thought you should know.

—Lionguard Hester

Dear %str1%,I discovered that gaeting crystals eventually turn into magnetite shards! I know you may be very worried, but rest assured, I spoke with the merchants throughout the Aerodrome and we all agreed to accept magnetite shards in place of gaeting crystals. Well, when you think about it, if there are no more gaeting crystals around, we all have to accept magnetite shards, right?Happy raiding!—Glenna, Durmand Priory Scholar

Dear %str1%,I hope all is well with you. Big update from the Well Kingdom of Winslow: I've recently amassed a new court. Before you ask, yes, they are rats. They're not the most talkative bunch, but they don't talk back to their regent, and that's what counts. That garden never worked out, so I've moved my efforts over to armoring. The Kingdom of Winslow endures. Lord Blaine, Prince of Winslow

Dear %str1%,I hope this finds you well. I'm afraid my efforts to get the queen to the summit haven't gone as smoothly as expected. The queen has had a situation arise that may prevent her from attending. I'll give you the full details when you arrive, but the short version is that someone is spreading rumors that Queen Jennah not only met with evil Scarlet prior to the Queen's Jubilee celebration, but also conspired to give Scarlet the mechanical watchknights. This is, of course, ridiculous. But the queen can't leave Divinity's Reach until these rumors are squelched. Countess Anise has suggested that I use my intuitive ability to find the cad spreading the rumors and discredit them. But, I don't want to do this alone. Will you come help me? Your friend,—Kasmeer

Dear %str1%,I reach out to you with the utmost urgency. It has been brought to my attention by two other members of Destiny's Edge that they're experiencing some trouble in Hoelbrak and the Black Citadel. Something, or someone, is burning both norn and charr out of their homes and forcing them to seek shelter and security in the cities. This is unprecedented, and I'm concerned. As a favor to my friends, I'm asking you to travel to one or both of these cities and see if you can offer any assistance. If this is as bad as they say, it may eventually threaten Krytan citizens as well. You're the perfect choice to deal with this. I'd help, but I have the queen's business to attend to.—Logan
Dear %str1%,I want to thank you for your help in Queen's Forest. Your reputation doesn't exaggerate your heroism and skill. All of us at the lumber mill thank you for your time. —Laborer Cardy

Dear %str1%,I'm dashing off a quick note from our camp on Thunder Ridge in Kessex Hills. Marjory has finally discovered an antitoxin to allow us into the Toxic Alliance's tower in Viathan Lake, and Marjory and I are setting out to learn its secrets.I'd feel a lot safer if I knew you were with us. Initial reports suggest that the enemy has an assortment of unique horrors waiting for us inside their tower.Be safe and well, please.—Lady Kasmeer Meade

Dear %str1%,I've finally achieved my lifelong dream of becoming a king. I'm still down here in the well, but surely my subjects above wouldn't forget about me, would they? I'll need some help when I make my grand emergence—it's been so long since I've seen light that I don't think my eyes, you know, function anymore. Make the appropriate arrangements for your lord, won't you? —His Majesty King Blaine, Regent of Winslow Well
Dear %str1%,I've had word that the Pale Tree will see us at our convenience. I'm heading there now with Rox and Braham. Please meet us when you can. We'll wait there for you.—Kasmeer

Dear %str1%,It's been some time, hasn't it? Remember me? It's Blaine! From the well! Things are going great for me down here. Not only did I stay safe in the White Mantle attack, but I'm making quite a life for myself in the well. Even adopted a pet rat! His name is Nibbles. He'll warm up to me soon, I just know it.Best regards,—BPS: I go by Blaine now. Figure its more appropriate for a man of my age.

Dear %str1%,Marjory and I have reached an impasse. For weeks, we've delivered injection after injection straight into the toxic heart of the Tower of Nightmares, but still it continues pulsing and corrupting the Kessex Hills region.We're not really sure what we're missing, but with Scarlet anything is possible. We urgently need you here with us. I've set up a mesmeric portal that will take you straight to us at the tower's heart chamber.Please, come quickly.—Lady Kasmeer Meade

Dear Arch Researcher Tunsi,    In celebration of the 99th anniversary of your natal egress, we are pleased to present you with a pineapple upside-down cake. Thank you for exhausting these last several decades of your life in pursuit of rigorous science and relentless innovation!    If you wish, you may let your staff eat cake during any downtime between experiments. Calculated from the prior decade's health examination logs, your odds of continued survival beyond the age of 100 diminish exponentially, so please enjoy this opportunity for rest and relaxation. In the words of our late Arch Headmaster Quag, "Science never stops, so naturally, then, neither can we."   As a reminder to staff, be sure to sign below to claim 20 minutes of cake time. This will ensure your compensation reductions are appropriately prorated.(Over a dozen titles and names are listed below. Most are illegible, and several names with lower-ranking titles have been crossed out.)Arch Researcher Tunsi  Senior Researcher Xhunn  Senior Energy Calculator Kagg  REDACTED Researcher Iga

Dear AUTO_REGISTERED_01,Thank you for registering your Xunlai Jade Bot! As part of our limited-time new-user giveaway, please find the enclosed research kit. You may use the research kit to deconstruct old crafted items to acquire valuable research notes. They can be used to craft new power cores and modules for your jade bot. Should you need another research kit in the future, one can be inexpensively purchased from a Xunlai technician near any jade bot workbench.Regards,Xunlai Jade Customer Relations

Dear Captain Magnus,I regret to inform you that I, Lieutenant Snargle Goldclaw, would like to formally resign from the Lionguard. As much as shining armor and acting as a vigilant protector of the realm roused my sense of pride, seeing the violence that ensued today has shattered my faith. At my core I feel an emptiness and loss... Blood, everywhere.    I have chosen another path. One that I know will supply the people with much-needed joy as they recover from the calamities that befell them. I am going to become...a writer. A writer of love and words that express love; hugs for the people, a kiss for the lost.    Good luck with the fights to come. While you attack our foes on foot, I will go for their hearts with the pen.  Snargle Goldclaw

Dear Commander,I have discovered an astounding property surrounding the Aurene-based weapons you carry. Similar to Aurene, they can absorb Elder Dragon magic, but the difference is that they change their form. Come speak with me at Aurene's Enclave in Seitung Province or at the Exchange in Arborstone, and we can get started on exploring what they can do. You'll need a flawless Elder Dragon scale to start, and I have a few I can part with in exchange for some resources.Sincerely,Leivas

Dear Commander,One of our secondborn, Cillian, has recently returned from their travels across Tyria and is quite eager to meet with you. Nothing too serious, mind, but they wished to consult with a storied and celebrated mesmer, and they could imagine no one better. If you happen to be available, you can find them in the Grove—just keep your ears open.—Dagonet

Dear Commander,Queen Jennah has invited constituents from Elona and Cantha to come teach our cultures to the people of Divinity's Reach. One of my guards, Nadeem, will be working with the Seraph on their seasonal training routines. He has expressed interest in meeting with you, warrior to warrior.You can find him in the Ossan Quarter in the city.Take care,High Councilor Imann

Dear Explorer,Tales of your deeds have traveled far and wide! However, these tales change with each retelling. To alleviate this, the Tyrian Explorers Society has adopted a new system. All tokens you received are now converted into Tales of Dungeon Delving. Rest assured, these Tales can be exchanged at the same rate as previous tokens at a local dungeon vendor. However, because this system has been unified, you may find yourself needing to re-explore these dungeons to access some of the goods these vendors possess.Happy delving!—Tyrian Explorers Society

Dear fan,As a supporter or participant in the events at Vehtendi Arena—Elona's most spectacular sports venue—please accept this message as recognition of your contribution to the games. Praise Joko!—MC Turgut

Dear Isla, Antonie, and Zora,Hello again, my loves. I feel as though I see you around every corner and behind every rock. Your voices giggle at the edge of my consciousness, just out of range. If only I could reach out and... But you pull away every time I get close.You all are the warmth of the sun on my skin and the icy shock of deep water, reminding me of what I once had and cruelly taunting me about what I've lost. Nothing ever changes on this island; all three of you still shimmer in my mind as you did on the last day I saw you. I cannot decide which is worse—that you will be forever frozen to me as you were then, or that you will change and grow old while I remain here, staring into the ceaseless waves.Watch over them for me, Isla.Yours forever, wherever the Mists take me,Nathaniel

Dear Mama,Happy birthday!Hope you like your gift. I made it with the fire spell you taught me. Don't worry, Papa helped a little. Next year, I'll make you an even better one! (If you teach me the stronger spell...just sayin...)We are excited to sellibrate with you!Love,Lan

Dear Pact Commander,Following the aftermath of the demonic attacks on our cities, political leaders of this world have decided to act. I would like to discuss the first summit of the Tyrian Alliance at your earliest convenience.Countess Anise and I will be waiting for you in Divinity's Reach.Sincerely,Queen Jennah of Kryta

Dear Serene Light,    I'll skip the pleasantries: I miss you. I miss you so much that it makes my soul ache, so much that it mutes every rainbow I see on this forsaken island to shades of gray.    There is no light, love, or laughter in this camp. Each morning, I'm rousted out of my leaking tent just to perform the same three tasks I did the day before: forage for food and water, organize and distribute supplies, and risk my neck on expeditions. I respect Vigilant Dawn for running this place like clockwork, but it feels more and more like a clock that's ticking toward my demise. I fear that the next time you see me, my love, I may be in pieces.    I hope all is well at home. Give your sister and her cubs my love. When I have a spare moment, I plan to climb to the highest peak I can find around here and look across the bay to you. I pray that perhaps if I look hard enough, I will find your gentle glow shining through the gloom.    All my love forever and always,    Nested Timber

Dear Wintersday revelers,My airship and I have returned to Divinity's Reach for another year of Wintersday merriment. Prepare yourself, for a plethora of holiday happenings await you!Speak with the Bell Maestro to make happy harmonics in the Bell Choir, or to the Festive Youth to jump into my Winter Wonderland.You may also converse with the Festive Lionguard near the Divinity's Reach snow globe for some Snowball Mayhem. And for those who desire a challenge, journey through the candy-swirl portal next to me for a test of skill inside my Infinirarium airship.I've also heard that the local orphanage has suffered the immodest tragedy of having its Wintersday ornaments stolen! The orphans could surely use your help retrieving them.And then there's the matter of the malfunctioning toys in my workshop. Left unchecked, they could ruin Wintersday! You can enter my ship and reduce the toys to scraps in Toypocalypse by speaking to the Toy Captain near me.As always, happy holidays,—Toymaker Tixx

Dearest %str1%, it is with great pleasure that quaggan writes you! You are quaggan's favorite now because you were so good about keeping the krait away. You inspired quaggans. One day, quaggans will throw the krait out of Nonmoa Lake, and quaggans will have a free and safe home. Love! —Commander Spleedoop

Dearest fellow coin enthusiasts,   I hope this letter reaches you all in the heartiest of health. For those who could not attend our latest symposium on unusual coins, I bear tidings of a recent change in categorization that you should all become immediately aware of.To be brief: your current collections of unusual coins have been recatagorized as ancient coins. I will still happily trade for your ancient coins as I once did when we called them "unusual." I will also gladly take any new unusual coins you may find in exchange for my ancient coins, so have no fear of missing out!The society has deemed that all coins in more recently discovered territories shall be called unusual coins until such time that they have been properly examined and catalogued, at which point they shall also be reclassified as ancient coins. Collectors of unusual coins will no doubt make themselves known in such locations and offer interesting new incentives to trade with them.This system of discovery and reclassification shall continue in perpetuity. Or until the society changes its mind. Thank you for your time, and I hope to see you all at the next symposium.—Chin-Hwa, Collector of Ancient Coins

Dedicated to Lord Faren, who pays generously.CHAPTER 1Deep in the Maguuma Jungle, the world had just been saved. Mordremoth had fallen...in love. Yes, friends, it's the truth: it was not swords that ended the war. For while the Pact commander had conquered the dragon's mind, Lord Faren had conquered the dragon's heart."No one can know that you still live," Faren said, a single tear flowing down his cheek. "They wouldn't believe in you the way I do.""I know, my shining knight." The Elder Dragon whispered back. "And yet, you must leave my side. For you have other lives to save...and more love to give."
